title: astragalus galegiformis
intro: Astragalus galegiformis, also known as Mongolian milkvetch, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of the Altai Mountains in Mongolia and southern Siberia. The roots of the plant are rich in saponins, flavonoids, and other compounds, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties, making it a valuable medicinal herb. Astragalus galegiformis is used to boost the immune system, reduce fatigue, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The plant is traditionally prepared as a decoction, tincture, or extract, and is also used in combination with other herbs in traditional Chinese medicine to enhance its medicinal properties.
